entity.description: Salmon is an excellent source of vitamin B12, vitamin D and selenium. It is a good source of niacin, omega-3 fatty acids, protein, phosphorus and vitamin B6. The salmon flesh texture are combination of flaky and smooth. It has a mild sweet flavour with strong briny taste. Salmon also taste great even just by baking in oven with olive oil without other seasoning.

displayMetadata.name: KEY INFORMATION
displayMetadata.value:
• Packed in the convenient pack where consumer can easily take the portion they want as each piece of salmon slice are packed individually
• Each packet consists of 4 pieces

displayMetadata.name: PREPARATION
displayMetadata.value:
Cooking instructions:
1. Take out salmon slice from the packet
2. Defrost it
3. Pour some oil into frying pan and heat it up
4. Place salmon slice into pan and fry about 3 to 5 minutes on each side
5. It is ready to be served

metaData.Preparation:
Cooking instructions:
The product must be fully thawed, drained and cooked thoroughly before consumption.
Bake
1. Preheat the oven to 200°C.
2. In a pan, brush the fish with melted butter and season with the spices of your choice.
3. Bake for 10-12 minutes or until the fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.

Pan fry
1. Preheat the pan and add a little oil.
2. Season the fish with your choice of seasoning.
3. Place the seasoned fish in the pan and cook over medium heat.
4. Cook each side for about 5-6 minutes or until the fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.

Cooking times may vary based on the thickness of the sutchi fillets.
metaData.SAP Product Name: OCEAN FRESH DELITE FROZEN SUTCHI FILLET 800G
metaData.Storage Information:
Keep frozen (-18°C & below).
Once thawed, do not refreeze.

metaData.Preparation:
Broiling or Grilling
1. Brush with olive oil on a broiler/grilling pan and preheat it.
2. Season the boiled scallops with salt and pepper, place them on the pan.
3. Broil or grill the boiled scallops until slightly brown.
4. Best served immediately

Pan Frying
1. Season the boiled scallops with salt and pepper.
2. Preheat a skillet and drizzle with olive oil on medium-high heat.
3. Place scallops in skillet and let each side cook for 3-5 minutes.
4. Best served immediately

Boiling/ Steamboat:
1. Add scallops into boiling soup for 2-3 minutes.
2. It is ready to be served.

product.description: French scallops are wild-caught from the Normandy and Brittany coasts of France and flash frozen at sea to retain their original flavour and plump texture. These scallops are tender with a sweet buttery flavour, and ideal for high-heat cooking such as soups and broths, searing, baking, broiling, frying and grilling.

metaData.Ingredients: Scallops (Pecten Maximus), Water and Humectants [Ins450(I), Ins451(Ii)].
metaData.Key Information: Plump in texture and buttery flavour
metaData.Preparation:
Scallops must be thawed and fully cooked before consumption. Scallops should be firm and opaque when cooked.

[Pan Sear Icon] Pan Sear:
1. Pat dry the scallops with kitchen paper and season them if necessary.
2. Preheat a pan over medium heat and add olive oil or butter.
3. Add the scallops and cook for 6 minutes (3 minutes on each side) or until golden browned.


[Pot Icon] Soup: Place the scallops in boiling soup and simmer till cooked.
metaData.Storage Information:
Keep frozen (-18°C & below).
Once thawed, do not refreeze.

metaData.Key Information: Our Nile fish White Fillet are a premium hand cut portion and are wholesome and 100% natural. It contains no preservatives nor additives and makes a perfect cuisine for broiled, fried, grilled, baked or steamed.
metaData.Preparation:
1. Defrost 3 pieces of Nile Fish Fillet (450g) and leave it aside
2. Combine 1/3 cup of all-purpose flour, 1/2 teapsoon of salt and 1/4 teaspoon of pepper in a shallow dish
3. Thoroughly dredge fillets with flour
4. Heat up 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat
5. Place fish in frying pan, work in batches if necessary
6. Cook 3 to 4 minutes per side until lightly browned
7. Ready to serve

metaData.Preparation:
1. Place the unopened bag of fish slices in cool water (below 25 °C) until the fish is fully thawed.
For soup and porridge
2. Remove the bag and add the fish slices to boiling water.
3. Keep the heat on and cook evenly for 30 seconds, then remove the fish slices from the water.

metaData.Preparation:
• Bake:
400°F for 15 minutes
• Grill:
3 minutes - 5 minutes on each side
• Broil:
Cooking time 5 minutes - 7 minutes
• Pan Fry:
3 minutes - 4 minutes on each side
• Note:
1. Brush with melted butter or oil and season to taste for best results
2. Cooking times may vary and fillets are done when fish flakes easily
• Thawing Instructions:
1. Submerge vaccum pack in cold water for 15 minutes
2. Once thawed, remove from packaging and use within 2 days
